summary: Admin Panel Permission Matrix issue investigation complete. ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED: 1) The seed endpoint was writing entities to wrong collection ('entities' instead of 'entity_configs') and using wrong field name ('name' instead of 'entity_name'). 2) The seed endpoint was creating permissions with 'entity_name' field instead of 'entity' field. 3) The production backend at https://lawfirm-ops.emergent.host is running OLD CODE that hasn't been updated with the fixes. The local backend has been fixed and tested successfully.

critical.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error on production. Root cause: permissions in database have 'entity_name' field but code expects 'entity' field
Returns empty array on production. Root cause: seed endpoint was writing to 'entities' collection instead of 'entity_configs' collection

integration_issues.issue: Shows 'No entities configured' and 'Error loading permissions' because backend APIs are failing

action_items: CRITICAL: Deploy the updated backend code to production. The fixes have been made to /app/backend/server.py and /app/backend/admin_routes.py
After deployment, call /api/seed-database on production to populate entity_configs collection
After deployment, call /api/migrate-permissions on production to fix existing permissions (change entity_name to entity field)
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: server.py seed endpoint now uses 'entity_configs' collection instead of 'entities'
FIXED: server.py seed endpoint now uses 'entity_name' field instead of 'name' for entities
FIXED: server.py seed endpoint now uses 'entity' field instead of 'entity_name' for permissions
FIXED: admin_routes.py get_role_permissions now handles both 'entity' and 'entity_name' fields for backward compatibility
ADDED: /api/migrate-permissions endpoint to fix existing permissions data
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py - Fixed seed endpoint to use correct collection and field names
/app/backend/admin_routes.py - Added backward compatibility for entity/entity_name field
seed_data_creation: Seed endpoint creates: 3 roles (Admin, Supervisor, Agent), 5 entities (leads, deposits, teams, reports, settings), and permissions for each role
context_for_next_testing_agent: The code fixes have been applied to the local backend but the production backend at https://lawfirm-ops.emergent.host is running OLD CODE. The production URL returns different data than localhost, confirming separate deployments. After deploying the updated code, run: 1) /api/seed-database to create entities, 2) /api/migrate-permissions to fix permissions. Local tests pass 100%.

issue_1_no_entities.root_cause: Seed endpoint was writing to 'entities' collection with 'name' field, but admin API reads from 'entity_configs' collection with 'entity_name' field
issue_1_no_entities.fix_applied: Changed seed endpoint to use 'entity_configs' collection and 'entity_name' field

issue_2_error_loading_permissions.symptom: Permission Matrix shows 'Error loading permissions' and API returns 500
issue_2_error_loading_permissions.root_cause: Seed endpoint created permissions with 'entity_name' field, but admin API expects 'entity' field (KeyError: 'entity')
issue_2_error_loading_permissions.fix_applied: 1) Changed seed endpoint to use 'entity' field, 2) Added backward compatibility in admin_routes.py to handle both fields, 3) Added migration endpoint

deployment_required.reason: Production backend at https://lawfirm-ops.emergent.host is running old code. Local backend has fixes but production hasn't been updated.
deployment_required.verification: curl https://lawfirm-ops.emergent.host/api/ returns version without '2026-01-12-fix-entities', while localhost returns with version
